method: PYTHONPATH shadow (throwaway git worktrees under /tmp/pt-verify-wt/{base,layout-sinking,mor-finalize}, prebuilt HEAD .so + generated artifacts symlinked in; both branch diffs are pure-python inductor) over torch 2.13.0a0 on B200. BASE arm = bare 5e2ab3055de shadow; BRANCH arm = PR branch tip shadow. Fresh TORCHINDUCTOR_CACHE_DIR per (arm,set). All timing via scripts/bench_parallel.py (locked GPU path, clock lock 1852MHz, --no-cd, min-of-rounds do_bench). Same repro set both arms; branch arms repeated (run2). Arm separation proven by output _metadata.pytorch_commit per arm (5e2ab3055de vs f56267540e4 vs 8d664ddc25e) and by structural codegen tells (counter/nk for U10, triton_mor_finalize_sum marker for U30).

pr/layout-sinking-clean.bench: all 14 flag-ablation-affected shufflenet_v2 repros (mega_ablation affected_U10.txt) --all-shapes = 18 shape points
pr/layout-sinking-clean.expected: ~1.20x kernel geomean (flag-ablation at HEAD measured flag-off/control = 1.2003)

measured.structural: n_kernels drops on 12/18 points (2->1, 4->1, 4->2, 6->4); 18/18 points faster, none regress

pr/layout-sinking-clean.expected_vs_measured_note: Measured 1.38x EXCEEDS the expected 1.20x. The 1.2003 flag-ablation was the MARGINAL contribution at HEAD daa79cd25ca, where other landed units (scalar-acc, Blackwell-BN heuristics, etc.) had already sped up these var_mean/pointwise kernels; against clean base 5e2ab the same pass removes more of the remaining gap.
pr/layout-sinking-clean.feature_proof: counters['inductor']['layout_transform_store_sinking']=1 on affected repros (0 on base arm); flag-off on the branch arm (TORCHINDUCTOR_LAYOUT_TRANSFORM_STORE_SINKING=0) restores base codegen exactly (njit 2->4, counter 0)
pr/layout-sinking-clean.verdict: REPRODUCES (exceeds: 1.38x vs expected 1.20x; every point faster)

pr/mor-finalize-clean.bench: 6 of the 52 affected sum_sum_sum repros (260a107eaf32 incl. its 7 model shape points, 11d45d703ba6 XLNet, 2261b2f5694a MobileBert, 0703a79bc871 mobilevit, 907cbb3d9f19 GoogleFnet, ecce309d13e3 LayoutLM) --all-shapes = 13 shape points

measured.outlier_investigation: sum_sum_sum_ecce309d13e3/LayoutLM read 0.72x in the batch A/B (base 330.6 vs branch 462.0), but the point is autotune-bistable: branch run2 in the same batch gave 386.9 (1.19x self-variance) and the BASE arm itself swings 330.6 -> 560.7/560.8 across fresh-cache runs (1.7x self-variance, 15-kernel graph, --no-cd). Isolated sequential re-bench, fresh caches, 2 runs per arm: base 560.7/560.8 vs branch 543.6/545.6 => branch FASTER 1.03x. No reproducible regression.
measured.structural: 12/13 points 1.04-1.44x faster; XLNet best 1.44x matches CORE_REVERT's documented best (1.46x, same repro)

pr/mor-finalize-clean.feature_proof: branch arm generated code contains 4 triton_mor_finalize_sum kernel defs on sum_sum_sum_260a107eaf32 (base 0); aggregate @triton.jit count 10 vs 6; kill switch TORCHINDUCTOR_TRITON_FINALIZE_SUM=0 on the branch arm restores base codegen exactly (0 markers, njit 6)
pr/mor-finalize-clean.verdict: REPRODUCES (1.17x as-measured vs expected 1.14x; the single negative reading does not survive re-measurement)

provenance.pushes_to_pytorch_remotes: none; branches pr/layout-sinking-clean (f56267540e4) and pr/mor-finalize-clean (8d664ddc25e) are local-only
provenance.artifacts: /tmp/scratch_space/pr56_verify/ (raw bench JSONs, smoke logs, compare.py, run_perf.sh, patches)
